indirect_fit_justification: Mayer Brown represents both creditors and debtors. Harrison leads International Enforcement & Asset Recovery — structurally aligned with cross-border gatekeeper enforcement mechanics. Outreach framed carefully as enforcement/creditor-side inquiry, not securities class action.
why_this_recipient: Harrison leads Mayer Brown's International Enforcement & Asset Recovery group — cross-border enforcement, resistance to judgment satisfaction, offshore debtor strategies. Directly relevant to Chinese-registered entity with no reachable assets.
why_now: Cross-border enforcement against offshore entities with no reachable assets is Harrison's practice mandate.
practice_match: International enforcement, asset recovery, cross-border judgment enforcement — strong structural match.
economic_match: Mayer Brown takes enforcement-side mandates. Hourly/hybrid model likely, but contingency discussions possible for right matter.
conflict_screen_note: Mayer Brown is mixed plaintiff/defense. Harrison leads the creditor/enforcement-side practice. Outreach framed as enforcement/creditor inquiry. Verify no conflict with gatekeeper targets before proceeding.

v1_issues: Language implied fault rather than assessment framing

outreach_draft.subject: Cross-Border Post-Judgment Enforcement — Offshore Entity — Recovery Assessment
outreach_draft.body:
Dear Mr. Harrison,

I am writing regarding a cross-border post-judgment enforcement matter that may align with Mayer Brown's international enforcement and asset recovery practice.

Our client holds an unsatisfied fraud judgment against a Chinese-registered offshore corporation. The judgment debtor has no reachable assets in accessible jurisdictions. The recovery path we are assessing involves potential claims or leverage against non-debtor gatekeepers — professional advisors and financial institutions whose role in the transaction or related conduct may warrant scrutiny.

The immediate objective is to assess whether viable recovery may be pursued against non-debtor gatekeepers, with early negotiated resolution preferred where feasible. The matter is only of interest if counsel sees a realistic path to material recovery against solvent non-debtor actors.

Mayer Brown's cross-border enforcement practice — specifically your experience structuring enforcement strategies when debtors resist judgment satisfaction through offshore relocation and asset shielding — reflects strong alignment with the mechanics this matter requires.

I would welcome a brief preliminary conversation. No commitment implied. Case details available upon request.

Kind regards,

Grant de Graf
CorporateXfactor
Special Situations / Post-Judgment Recovery

Website: corporatexfactor.com
Tel 212-444-1813
